Administrative & Clinical Processes.label: Utilization Management
Administrative & Clinical Processes.definition: Techniques used by health plans to manage healthcare costs by influencing patient care decision-making through case-by-case assessments.

Administrative & Clinical Processes.label: Appeals Process
Administrative & Clinical Processes.definition: The formal procedure for a patient or provider to request a review of a denied health insurance claim or prior authorization.

Administrative & Clinical Processes.label: Peer-to-Peer Review
Administrative & Clinical Processes.definition: A conversation between a treating physician and a medical reviewer from a health plan, typically following a denied prior authorization.

Administrative & Clinical Processes.label: Electronic Prescribing
Administrative & Clinical Processes.definition: The electronic generation and transmission of a prescription order from a provider to a pharmacy.

Administrative & Clinical Processes.label: Gold Carding
Administrative & Clinical Processes.definition: A policy that exempts high-performing clinicians from prior authorization requirements for certain services.

Administrative & Clinical Processes.label: Value-Based Care
Administrative & Clinical Processes.definition: A healthcare delivery model where providers are paid based on patient health outcomes rather than the volume of services provided.

Administrative & Clinical Processes.label: Information Blocking
Administrative & Clinical Processes.definition: A practice by a health IT developer, HIE, or provider that is likely to interfere with the access or use of electronic health information.

Administrative & Clinical Processes.label: Step Therapy
Administrative & Clinical Processes.definition: A type of prior authorization where a patient must try one or more lower-cost medications before a more expensive drug is covered.

Administrative & Clinical Processes.label: Revenue Cycle Management
Administrative & Clinical Processes.definition: The financial process using medical billing software that healthcare facilities use to track patient care episodes from registration to final payment.

Administrative & Clinical Processes.label: Real-Time Benefit Check
Administrative & Clinical Processes.definition: A process that allows providers to view patient-specific drug benefit and cost information at the point of care.

Administrative & Clinical Processes.label: Concurrent Review
Administrative & Clinical Processes.definition: A utilization management process that occurs during a patient's hospital stay to determine if continued care is medically necessary.

Administrative & Clinical Processes.label: Medical Necessity Review
Administrative & Clinical Processes.definition: The process of determining if a healthcare service or supply is reasonable and necessary for the diagnosis or treatment of illness.

Data & Document Types.label: Healthcare Attachment
Data & Document Types.definition: Supplemental information, such as clinical documentation, provided to support an administrative healthcare transaction.

Data & Document Types.label: C-CDA
Data & Document Types.definition: Consolidated Clinical Document Architecture, an HL7 standard for creating clinical documents like discharge summaries and progress notes.

Data & Document Types.label: FHIR Document
Data & Document Types.definition: A self-contained package of FHIR resources that represents a snapshot in time of a clinical document.

Data & Document Types.label: Certified EHR Technology
Data & Document Types.definition: An Electronic Health Record that meets the technological capability, functionality, and security requirements adopted by HHS.

Data & Document Types.label: Formulary
Data & Document Types.definition: A list of prescription drugs covered by a prescription drug plan or another insurance plan offering prescription drug benefits.

Data & Document Types.label: Advanced EOB
Data & Document Types.definition: An Advanced Explanation of Benefits, provided to a patient in advance of a scheduled service.

Data & Document Types.label: Protected Health Information
Data & Document Types.definition: Individually identifiable health information that is transmitted or maintained in any form or medium.

Data & Document Types.label: Good Faith Estimate
Data & Document Types.definition: An estimate of the cost of medical services for uninsured or self-pay individuals.

Data & Document Types.label: Patient-Generated Health Data
Data & Document Types.definition: Health-related data created, recorded, or gathered by or from patients to help address a health concern.

Government Agencies & Bodies.label: HHS
Government Agencies & Bodies.definition: The U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, a federal agency protecting the health of all Americans.
terms: Department of Health and Human Services

Government Agencies & Bodies.label: HITAC
Government Agencies & Bodies.definition: The Health Information Technology Advisory Committee, which advises ONC on health IT policies, standards, and implementation specifications.

Government Agencies & Bodies.label: OIG
Government Agencies & Bodies.definition: The Office of Inspector General for HHS, which combats waste, fraud, and abuse in HHS programs.

Government Agencies & Bodies.label: FDA
Government Agencies & Bodies.definition: The Food and Drug Administration, a federal agency responsible for protecting public health by regulating food and drug products.

Government Agencies & Bodies.label: NCI
Government Agencies & Bodies.definition: The National Cancer Institute, the U.S. government's principal agency for cancer research and training.

Government Agencies & Bodies.label: NCVHS
Government Agencies & Bodies.definition: The National Committee on Vital and Health Statistics, a federal advisory body to HHS on health data and statistics.
terms: National Committee on Vital and Health Statistics

Government Agencies & Bodies.label: AHRQ
Government Agencies & Bodies.definition: The Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality, a federal agency focused on improving healthcare quality, safety, and efficiency.
terms: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality

Government Agencies & Bodies.label: NIST
Government Agencies & Bodies.definition: The National Institute of Standards and Technology, a federal agency promoting U.S. innovation by advancing measurement science and standards.
terms: National Institute of Standards and Technology

Healthcare Programs & Plans.label: Medicare
Healthcare Programs & Plans.definition: A federal health insurance program for people 65 or older, and younger people with certain disabilities.

Healthcare Programs & Plans.label: Medicaid
Healthcare Programs & Plans.definition: A joint federal and state program that helps with medical costs for some people with limited income and resources.

Healthcare Programs & Plans.label: Medicare Advantage
Healthcare Programs & Plans.definition: A type of Medicare health plan offered by a private company that contracts with Medicare to provide Part A and Part B benefits.

Healthcare Programs & Plans.label: Medicare Part D
Healthcare Programs & Plans.definition: The part of Medicare that provides prescription drug coverage.

Healthcare Programs & Plans.label: Medicaid MCO
Healthcare Programs & Plans.definition: A Medicaid Managed Care Organization, a private health plan that contracts with state Medicaid agencies.

Healthcare Programs & Plans.label: CHIP
Healthcare Programs & Plans.definition: The Children's Health Insurance Program, providing low-cost health coverage to children in families who earn too much for Medicaid.

Healthcare Programs & Plans.label: Prescription Drug Plan
Healthcare Programs & Plans.definition: A standalone plan that provides prescription drug coverage, typically associated with Medicare Part D.

Healthcare Programs & Plans.label: Medicare Part B
Healthcare Programs & Plans.definition: The part of Medicare that covers medical services and supplies, including outpatient care and durable medical equipment.

Healthcare Programs & Plans.label: Qualified Health Plan
Healthcare Programs & Plans.definition: An insurance plan that is certified by the Health Insurance Marketplace, provides essential health benefits, and follows established limits.

Legislation & Regulations.label: HIPAA
Legislation & Regulations.definition: The Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act, setting national standards for protecting sensitive patient health information.
terms: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act

Legislation & Regulations.label: 21st Century Cures Act
Legislation & Regulations.definition: A U.S. law designed to accelerate medical product development and bring new innovations to patients faster and more efficiently.

Legislation & Regulations.label: Affordable Care Act
Legislation & Regulations.definition: A comprehensive healthcare reform law enacted in 2010 to make affordable health insurance available to more people.

Legislation & Regulations.label: PAMA
Legislation & Regulations.definition: The Protecting Access to Medicare Act, which includes provisions for appropriate use criteria for advanced diagnostic imaging.

Legislation & Regulations.label: HITECH Act
Legislation & Regulations.definition: The Health Information Technology for Economic and Clinical Health Act, promoting the adoption and meaningful use of health IT.
terms: Health Information Technology for Economic and Clinical Health Act

Legislation & Regulations.label: ERISA
Legislation & Regulations.definition: The Employee Retirement Income Security Act, a federal law setting minimum standards for most voluntarily established retirement and health plans.

Legislation & Regulations.label: SUPPORT Act
Legislation & Regulations.definition: A law aimed at addressing the opioid crisis, including requirements for electronic prior authorization for Medicare Part D.

Legislation & Regulations.label: 42 CFR Part 2
Legislation & Regulations.definition: A federal regulation that provides strict confidentiality protections for patient records related to substance use disorder treatment.

Legislation & Regulations.label: Medicare Modernization Act
Legislation & Regulations.definition: A 2003 law that introduced prescription drug benefits (Part D) and Medicare Advantage plans.

Medical Conditions & Diseases.label: Cancer
Medical Conditions & Diseases.definition: A disease in which some of the body's cells grow uncontrollably and spread to other parts of the body.

Medical Conditions & Diseases.label: Chronic Condition
Medical Conditions & Diseases.definition: A health condition or disease that is persistent or otherwise long-lasting in its effects.

Medical Conditions & Diseases.label: Prostate Cancer
Medical Conditions & Diseases.definition: Cancer that occurs in the prostate — a small walnut-shaped gland in males.

Medical Conditions & Diseases.label: Diabetes
Medical Conditions & Diseases.definition: A chronic health condition that affects how your body turns food into energy.

Medical Conditions & Diseases.label: HIV
Medical Conditions & Diseases.definition: Human immunodeficiency virus, a virus that attacks the body's immune system.

Medical Conditions & Diseases.label: Cardiovascular Disease
Medical Conditions & Diseases.definition: A general term for conditions affecting the heart or blood vessels.

Medical Conditions & Diseases.label: Inflammatory Bowel Disease
Medical Conditions & Diseases.definition: A term for two conditions (Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis) that are characterized by chronic inflammation of the gastrointestinal tract.

Medical Conditions & Diseases.label: Parkinson's Disease
Medical Conditions & Diseases.definition: A progressive disorder that affects the nervous system and the parts of the body controlled by the nerves.

Medical Items & Services.label: Imaging Services
Medical Items & Services.definition: Medical tests that produce pictures of the inside of the body.

Medical Items & Services.label: Outpatient Services
Medical Items & Services.definition: Medical care that does not require an overnight stay in a hospital or facility.

Medical Items & Services.label: Inpatient Services
Medical Items & Services.definition: Medical services that require a patient to be admitted to a hospital or other facility for at least one night.

Medical Items & Services.label: Durable Medical Equipment
Medical Items & Services.definition: Equipment and supplies ordered by a health care provider for everyday or extended use.

Medical Items & Services.label: Post-Acute Care
Medical Items & Services.definition: A range of medical care services that support an individual's continued recovery from an illness or management of a chronic condition.

Medical Items & Services.label: Chemotherapy
Medical Items & Services.definition: A type of cancer treatment that uses one or more anti-cancer drugs as part of a standardized regimen.

Medical Items & Services.label: Specialty Medication
Medical Items & Services.definition: High-cost prescription medications used to treat complex or chronic conditions.

Medical Items & Services.label: Radiation Therapy
Medical Items & Services.definition: A type of cancer treatment that uses high doses of radiation to kill cancer cells and shrink tumors.

Medical Items & Services.label: Inpatient Rehabilitation Facility
Medical Items & Services.definition: A facility that provides intensive rehabilitation services to patients after an injury, illness, or surgery.

Medical Specialties & Roles.label: Specialist
Medical Specialties & Roles.definition: A physician who concentrates on a specific area of medicine.

Medical Specialties & Roles.label: Oncologist
Medical Specialties & Roles.definition: A physician who treats cancer and provides medical care for a person diagnosed with cancer.

Medical Specialties & Roles.label: Pharmacist
Medical Specialties & Roles.definition: A healthcare professional who specializes in the right way to use, store, preserve, and provide medicine.

Medical Specialties & Roles.label: Physical Therapist
Medical Specialties & Roles.definition: A healthcare professional who diagnoses and treats individuals with medical problems that limit their abilities to move and function.

Medical Specialties & Roles.label: Radiologist
Medical Specialties & Roles.definition: A medical doctor that specializes in diagnosing and treating injuries and diseases using medical imaging procedures.

Medical Specialties & Roles.label: Occupational Therapist
Medical Specialties & Roles.definition: A healthcare professional who helps people with injuries or disabilities participate in the things they want and need to do.

Medical Specialties & Roles.label: Primary Care Physician
Medical Specialties & Roles.definition: A physician who provides both the first contact for a person with an undiagnosed health concern and continuing care.

Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.label: HL7
Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.definition: Health Level Seven International, a not-for-profit, standards-developing organization for electronic health information.

Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.label: AMA
Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.definition: The American Medical Association, a professional association and lobbying group for physicians and medical students.

Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.label: Da Vinci Project
Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.definition: An HL7 FHIR Accelerator project that helps payers and providers implement value-based care data exchange.

Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.label: NCPDP
Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.definition: The National Council for Prescription Drug Programs, a standards development organization for the pharmacy services sector.
terms: National Council for Prescription Drug Programs

Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.label: CAQH
Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.definition: The Council for Affordable Quality Healthcare, a non-profit alliance that creates shared initiatives to streamline healthcare administration.

Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.label: PBM
Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.definition: A Pharmacy Benefit Manager, a third-party administrator of prescription drug programs for commercial health plans and other payers.

Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.label: CodeX
Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.definition: An HL7 FHIR Accelerator focused on improving cancer care and research through interoperable data standards.

Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.label: WEDI
Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.definition: The Workgroup for Electronic Data Interchange, an association that is an advisor to HHS on healthcare information technology.

Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.label: CARIN Alliance
Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.definition: A multi-sector alliance working to advance consumer-directed exchange of health information.

Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.label: Accredited Standards Committee X12
Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.definition: The organization that develops and maintains the X12 EDI standards for administrative healthcare transactions.

Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.label: HIE
Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.definition: A Health Information Exchange, which allows doctors, nurses, and other health care providers to securely share patient medical information.

Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.label: ACO
Organizations & Alliances (Non-Governmen.definition: An Accountable Care Organization, a group of healthcare providers who agree to be accountable for the quality, cost, and experience of care.

Standards & Technologies.label: FHIR
Standards & Technologies.definition: Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources, a standard for exchanging healthcare information electronically.

Standards & Technologies.label: X12 Standard
Standards & Technologies.definition: A set of standards for electronic data interchange (EDI) for administrative health care transactions.

Standards & Technologies.label: X12 278
Standards & Technologies.definition: The HIPAA-mandated transaction standard for healthcare service review requests and responses, including prior authorizations.

Standards & Technologies.label: Da Vinci PAS IG
Standards & Technologies.definition: The Da Vinci Project's FHIR Implementation Guide for submitting prior authorization requests and receiving responses.

Standards & Technologies.label: Da Vinci CRD IG
Standards & Technologies.definition: The Da Vinci Project's FHIR Implementation Guide for discovering payer coverage requirements for services and medications.

Standards & Technologies.label: CDS Hooks
Standards & Technologies.definition: An HL7 specification for invoking external clinical decision support services from within an electronic health record workflow.

Standards & Technologies.label: NCPDP SCRIPT
Standards & Technologies.definition: A standard from the National Council for Prescription Drug Programs for transmitting prescription information electronically.

Standards & Technologies.label: CQL
Standards & Technologies.definition: Clinical Quality Language, a high-level, domain-specific language focused on the expression of clinical quality measures.

Standards & Technologies.label: SMART on FHIR
Standards & Technologies.definition: A set of open specifications to integrate third-party applications with electronic health records and other health IT systems.

Standards & Technologies.label: X12 275
Standards & Technologies.definition: The HIPAA transaction standard for electronically transmitting additional information, or attachments, to support a healthcare claim.

Standards & Technologies.label: CPT Codes
Standards & Technologies.definition: Current Procedural Terminology codes used to report medical, surgical, and diagnostic procedures and services.

Standards & Technologies.label: Da Vinci DTR IG
Standards & Technologies.definition: The Da Vinci Project's FHIR Implementation Guide for retrieving payer documentation templates and rules.
terms: Documentation Templates and Rules Implementation Guide
Da Vinci Documentation Templates and Rules

Standards & Technologies.label: USCDI
Standards & Technologies.definition: The United States Core Data for Interoperability, a standardized set of health data classes and elements for nationwide exchange.
terms: United States Core Data for Interoperability

Standards & Technologies.label: mCODE
Standards & Technologies.definition: The minimal Common Oncology Data Elements, a FHIR implementation guide for a standard oncology data language.

Standards & Technologies.label: ICD-10
Standards & Technologies.definition: International Classification of Diseases, Tenth Revision, a medical classification list for diagnoses.
terms: International Classification of Diseases, Tenth Revision

Standards & Technologies.label: LOINC
Standards & Technologies.definition: Logical Observation Identifiers Names and Codes, a standard for identifying medical laboratory observations.
terms: Logical Observation Identifiers Names and Codes

Standards & Technologies.label: US Core IG
Standards & Technologies.definition: An HL7 FHIR implementation guide defining the minimum conformance requirements for accessing patient data in the U.S.

Standards & Technologies.label: Da Vinci CDex IG
Standards & Technologies.definition: The Da Vinci Project's FHIR Implementation Guide for exchanging clinical data between payers and providers.

Standards & Technologies.label: NPI
Standards & Technologies.definition: National Provider Identifier, a unique 10-digit identification number issued to health care providers in the United States.

Standards & Technologies.label: HCPCS
Standards & Technologies.definition: Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System, a set of codes representing procedures, products, and services.

Standards & Technologies.label: NDC
Standards & Technologies.definition: National Drug Code, a unique product identifier for human drugs in the United States.

Standards & Technologies.label: SNOMED CT
Standards & Technologies.definition: Systematized Nomenclature of Medicine - Clinical Terms, a comprehensive, multilingual clinical healthcare terminology.
terms: Systematized Nomenclature of Medicine - Clinical Terms
